title: राहु स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री राहु स्तोत्रम् ॥

अर्धकायं महावीर्यं चन्द्रादित्यविमर्दनम्।
सिंहिकागर्भसम्भूतं तं राहुं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ १॥

कृष्णवर्णं शिरोमात्रं खड्गचर्मधरं बलम्।
सर्पवाहनसंस्थानं प्रणमामि ग्रहेश्वरम्॥ २॥

छायाग्रहमीशानं गोमेदधारणप्रियम्।
उड़ददालप्रियं नित्यं प्रणमामि राहुं सदा॥ ३॥

सर्पभयनिवारकं गण्डमूलदोषहरम्।
कालसर्पदोषशमनं प्रणमामि राहुं गुरुम्॥ ४॥

नवग्रहेषु तेजस्वी छायाग्रहपतिर्महान्।
सर्वग्रहभयच्छेदी प्रणमामि राहुं प्रभुम्॥ ५॥

राहुस्तोत्रमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेच्छनिवासरे।
राहुपीडा न बाधेत सर्वदोषनिवारणम्॥

॥ इति श्री राहु स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
benefits: Sacred Rahu Stotram for devotion and spiritual growth
Invokes blessings of Rahu in all forms
Invokes divine protection and planetary peace
Traditionally recited for removal of afflictions and bestows auspiciousness
Ideal for Rahu graha shanti and worship
description: The Rahu Stotram, known in Sanskrit as राहु स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of the Nine Planets (Navagraha). Rooted in Purana Tradition, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to invoke blessings of Rahu in all forms as well as remove afflictions and bestow auspiciousness. Additionally, it is known to grant divine protection and planetary peace. This prayer is traditionally recited on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Shani Amavasya to invoke divine grace. The text contains 5 verses and takes around 3 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Rahu Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Rahu Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn for Rahu graha shanti.

seoFaqs.question: How is this Rahu Stotram different from the longer Rahu Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: This shorter variant of the Rahu Stotram is a compact recitation that shares the opening verse Ardhakayam mahaviryam chandraditya-vimardanam with the longer Skanda Purana Rahu Stotra but condenses the descriptive verses into a five to six shloka format. It adds explicit references to Rahu as krishna-varna shiromatra (black-coloured head only), khadga-charma-dharam (holding sword and shield) and sarpa-vahana (serpent-mounted), and closes with a brief phalashruti rather than the elaborate one of the longer text. It is preferred for daily three-minute Saturday recitation while the longer Skanda Purana version is reserved for elaborate navagraha homas. (Sources: Stotra Samhita; Vedadhara Rahu texts.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is gomeda (hessonite) the gemstone of Rahu?
seoFaqs.answer: In jyotisha-ratna shastra each navagraha has an assigned gemstone whose colour and crystal structure resonate with the planetary frequency. Hessonite garnet (gomeda or gomedhikam) is assigned to Rahu because of its smoky-honey to blue-tinted colour resembling Rahu's chhaya-varna, and because hessonite is traditionally believed to absorb negative shadow-energies. The stone must be a minimum of three to six carats, set in silver or panchaloha (never gold), worn on the middle finger of the working hand after a Saturday prana-pratishtha during Rahu hora. The Rahu Stotram is chanted 108 times during the installation puja. Synthetic or treated stones are considered ineffective. (Sources: BV Raman Gemstones; AstroSage Rahu remedies.)

seoFaqs.question: What is Rahu Kalam and why are auspicious activities avoided in it?
seoFaqs.answer: Rahu Kalam is the daily Rahu hour, a 90-minute window that occurs at a different time on each day of the week — Sunday 4:30-6:00 pm, Monday 7:30-9:00 am, Tuesday 3:00-4:30 pm, Wednesday 12:00-1:30 pm, Thursday 1:30-3:00 pm, Friday 10:30 am-12:00 pm, Saturday 9:00-10:30 am (timings shift slightly with location). South Indian tradition strongly avoids commencing new ventures, journeys, marriages, business deals, or auspicious rituals during Rahu Kalam. However, paradoxically, Rahu pacification recitations including the Rahu Stotram are considered most effective when chanted during Rahu Kalam itself, since the planetary energy is most receptive at that time. (Sources: Drikpanchang Rahu Kalam calculator; AstroSage.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Tirunageswaram Rahu sthala in the Navagraha temple circuit?
seoFaqs.answer: Tirunageswaram, near Kumbakonam in Tamil Nadu, is one of the nine Navagraha temples (Navagraha Sthalam) of the Chola country and is the dedicated sthala of Rahu. The presiding deity is Naganatha Swami (Shiva) but Rahu is enshrined in his own sannidhi and the special Rahu Kalam abhishekam is the famed ritual — milk poured over the Rahu murti turns visibly blue during the abhishekam, attracting devotees from across the world. Pilgrims chant the Rahu Stotram during the abhishekam and offer wheat, blue lotus, urad dal vada and gomeda. The temple is the most powerful pilgrimage centre for Rahu-dosha pacification. (Sources: Tirunageswaram temple website; Wikipedia Navagraha Temples.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the karakatva (significations) of Rahu in Vedic astrology?
seoFaqs.answer: Rahu is the karaka of foreign lands, foreigners, unconventional desires, taboo subjects, technology, mass media, photography, electricity, aviation, snakes, poison, addictions, intoxicants, paternal grandfather, sudden reversals, scandal, espionage, hidden enemies, eclipses, materialistic ambition, and karmic patterns from past lives. He governs the Ardra, Swati and Shatabhisha nakshatras. A well-placed Rahu produces meteoric rises, foreign success and original thinkers; an afflicted Rahu causes addictions, scandals, mental confusion, chronic mysterious illness, and Kaal Sarp Dosh. The Rahu Stotram is recited specifically to convert the malefic dimensions of these karakatva into beneficial ones. (Sources: BV Raman Vedic Astrology; AstroSage Rahu karakatva.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Rahu connected to Naga worship and Naga Panchami?
seoFaqs.answer: Although Rahu is technically a chhaya-graha and not a serpent deity, jyotisha and folk tradition identify him strongly with the naga-loka because his vahana is the serpent and his ardhakaya form resembles a serpent's hood. Therefore Naga Panchami (Shravana Shukla Panchami), Naga Chaturthi and Subramanya Shashti are considered Rahu-pacification days. Devotees perform abhisheka with milk to naga statues, offer turmeric and kumkum, and chant the Rahu Stotram together with the Naga Stotram. Snake-bite victims, those with sarpa-dosha in horoscopes, and women seeking children (where Rahu afflicts the fifth house) particularly observe these days. (Sources: Drikpanchang Naga Panchami; AstroSage Sarpa Dosh.)
